t See udp-socket for user-level documentation. Below are notes about the module. Implementation details The implementation of a JavaScript module for UDPSocket will look like the dgram ( https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html ) module in node.js. We used

s.org/api/dgram.html ) module in node.js. We used DatagramSocket ( http://vertx.io/core_manual_java.html#user-datagram-protocol-udpl ) in Vert.x. Important notes This accessor is still under development, now it only supports listening to a port. Ther

nt, now it only supports listening to a port. There is a working demo under $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/udpSocket/demo. Resources for the demo You can forward incoming UDP packets on the specified port of SwarmBox to your local machine (to th

fied port of SwarmBox to your local machine (to the specified IP address) using the following JavaScript source code. (udpforward.js) (modified code from https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html ) var dgram = require ( "dgram" ) ; var server = dgram. create

= require ( "dgram" ) ; var server = dgram. createSocket ( "udp4" ) ; server. on ( "error" , function ( err ) { console. log ( "server error: \n " + err. stack ) ; server. close ( ) ; } ) ; server. on ( "message" , function ( msg , rinfo ) { console.

( "message" , function ( msg , rinfo ) { console. log ( "server got: " + msg + " from " + rinfo. address + ":" + rinfo. port + ' length: ' + msg. length ) ; client. send ( msg , 0 , msg. length , 22000 , "192.168.1.213" ) ; } ) ; server. on ( "liste

00 , "192.168.1.213" ) ; } ) ; server. on ( "listening" , function ( ) { var address = server. address ( ) ; console. log ( "server listening " + address. address + ":" + address. port ) ; } ) ; server. bind ( 22000 ) ; [$[Get Code]] Running an examp

S_NODE } / TestUDPSocketSelf [$[Get Code]] Running an example in Node by hand Below is another way to run the example. An example is available in the accessors repo at: /accessors/web/net/test/auto/TestUdpSocketSelf.js The example is a composite acce

stUdpSocketSelf.js The example is a composite accessor that sends and receives strings, which has been generated from the Cape Code model in the Ptolemy II repo at: $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/udpSocket/test/auto/TestUDPSocketSelf.xml To run

/udpSocket/test/auto/TestUDPSocketSelf.xml To run in node, open a command window, change directory to /accessors/web/hosts/node and start up the nodeHostShell: node nodeHostShell.js [$[Get Code]] In the shell, instantiate the example accessor: var a

he shell, instantiate the example accessor: var a = instantiate ( 'test' , 'net/test/auto/TestUdpSocketSelf' ) ; [$[Get Code]] Then, initialize the accessor: a. initialize ( ) ; [$[Get Code]] You should see the following output: undefined nsh > a1 a2

ld see the following output: undefined nsh > a1 a2 a3 a4 a5 a6 a7 a8 a9 a10 [$[Get Code]] See Also UDPSocketListener Accessor UDPSocketSender Accessor Watches , which could use this accessor $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/test/auto/{UDPSocketInt.xml.U

/terraswarm/accessor/test/auto/{UDPSocketInt.xml.UDPSocketString.xml} - two Cape Code Host tests $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/udpSocket/test/auto/TestUDPSocketSelf.xml - A Cape Code Host test that can generate a CompositeAccessor for the Node

hat can generate a CompositeAccessor for the Node Host accessors/web/net/test/TestUdpSocketSelf.js - A Composite Accessor version of the Cape Code Host that can be run by the Node Host Modules - Other Modules Edit - History - Print - Recent Changes -
